name: create_key_namespace_disagrees_with_obj
code:
proof fn phi_1_create_key_namespace_disagrees_with_obj(req: CreateRequest)
    requires
        req.obj.metadata.namespace is Some,
        req.namespace != req.obj.metadata.namespace->0,
    ensures
        req.key().namespace != req.obj.metadata.namespace->0,
{
}
reason: CreateRequest::key uses self.namespace while the obj carries its own metadata.namespace — if these can differ, the key does not faithfully represent the object's actual namespace

name: interference_check_ignores_uid
code:
proof fn phi_2_interference_check_ignores_uid(
    req: GetThenDeleteRequest, vrs1: VReplicaSetView, vrs2: VReplicaSetView
)
    requires
        vrs1.metadata.name == vrs2.metadata.name,
        vrs1.metadata.namespace == vrs2.metadata.namespace,
        vrs1.metadata.uid != vrs2.metadata.uid,
        vrs_reconcile_get_then_delete_request_only_interferes_with_itself(req, vrs1)(arbitrary()),
    ensures
        vrs_reconcile_get_then_delete_request_only_interferes_with_itself(req, vrs2)(arbitrary()),
{
}
reason: the interference predicate checks owner_ref.name and kind but not uid — two distinct VReplicaSets with the same name/namespace would satisfy each other's interference check, breaking isolation

name: create_interference_ignores_uid
code:
proof fn phi_3_create_interference_ignores_uid(
    req: CreateRequest, vrs1: VReplicaSetView, vrs2: VReplicaSetView, s: ClusterState
)
    requires
        vrs1.metadata.name == vrs2.metadata.name,
        vrs1.metadata.namespace == vrs2.metadata.namespace,
        vrs1.metadata.uid != vrs2.metadata.uid,
        vrs_reconcile_create_request_only_interferes_with_itself(req, vrs1)(s),
    ensures
        vrs_reconcile_create_request_only_interferes_with_itself(req, vrs2)(s),
{
}
reason: like the delete case, create interference checks owner_ref.name but not uid — a recreated VRS with the same name but different uid would pass the check, allowing cross-instance interference

name: lifted_action_trivially_holds_on_stuttering
reason: lifted_vrs checks the state predicate on both s and s_prime independently — on a stuttering execution this is trivially satisfied, meaning the action formulation adds no transition-specific constraint beyond the state invariant

name: object_ref_name_eq_ignores_uid
code:
proof fn phi_5_object_ref_name_eq_ignores_uid(vrs1: VReplicaSetView, vrs2: VReplicaSetView)
    requires
        vrs1.metadata.name == vrs2.metadata.name,
        vrs1.metadata.namespace == vrs2.metadata.namespace,
        vrs1.metadata.uid != vrs2.metadata.uid,
    ensures
        vrs1.object_ref() == vrs2.object_ref(),
{
}
reason: object_ref is determined solely by kind/name/namespace and ignores uid — two distinct resources (different uid) with the same name produce identical ObjectRefs, which can conflate identities throughout the spec
